parameters.name: initialize
parameters.label: Initialize Actor
parameters.description: Recreates the collision shapes for all the bodies in the Actor . Also resets all velocities and position to their default state. Initialize Actor should be pulsed when any changes are made to the SOPs

parameters.name: updatecs
parameters.label: Update Collision Shape
parameters.description: If enabled the Actor will automatically update collision shapes. This will occur when the "Collision SOPs" or "Collision Shape" parameters are changes or the underlying SOPs used to create the collisi

parameters.name: updatecspulse
parameters.label: Update Collision Shape
parameters.description: When clicked this will instantly update the collosion shape.

parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Toggle the actor on/off. If the actor is active, then it will be updated as the simulation progress. However, if it is inactive, then it will be removed from the simulation and no longer collide with

parameters.name: kinstate
parameters.label: Kinematic State
parameters.description: The kinematic state defines the Actor COMPs ability to move from external forces. If an object is dynamic, then it is moveable in the simulation, but if it static then it is not.

parameters.name: sops
parameters.label: Collision SOPs
parameters.description: Specifies SOPs or COMPs to use for the collision shape. If a is referenced, then just that will be used for the collision shape. But if a is selected then all SOPs inside of that (recursive) will be u

parameters.name: shape
parameters.label: Collision Shape
parameters.description: The type of collision shape to make from the selected SOPs. Collision shapes can be viewed using a guide in the Actor 's viewer

parameters.name: elltol
parameters.label: Ellipsoid Tolerance
parameters.description: The tolerance of the minimum volume bounding ellipsoid. In other words, how close to the optimal solution it is.

parameters.name: infinitemass
parameters.label: Infinite Mass
parameters.description: Give the actor infinite mass. If the object is dynamic this will make it unmovable and static. Toggling infinite mass on or off will not require recreation of the collision shape, unlike changing the

parameters.name: mass
parameters.label: Mass
parameters.description: The mass in kilograms of the actor.

parameters.name: cuevel
parameters.label: Cue Velocity
parameters.description: Holds the linear and angular velocity and values given by linvel and angvel. The object will still collide with any other bodies in the simulation.

parameters.name: cuevelpulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: the linear and angular velocity to values given by linvel and angvel. This will set the velocity to the given value at the beginning of the next frame.

parameters.name: forces
parameters.label: Forces
parameters.description: A list of local forces, meaning forces (ie. Force COMPs) that will only be applied to this actor.

parameters.name: globalgrav
parameters.label: Use Global Gravity
parameters.description: Toggle for whether to use the Bullet Solver 's gravity (global), or its own local gravity.

parameters.name: friction
parameters.label: Friction
parameters.description: The kinetic friction of the actor. It is the resistance between two bodies rubbing/sliding. The overall friction is the product of the two bodies touching. For example, if one body has 0 friction and

parameters.name: rollfric
parameters.label: Rolling Friction
parameters.description: The rolling friction of the actor. It is the resistance/drag of one body (such as a sphere or cone) rolling on another.

parameters.name: rest
parameters.label: Restitution
parameters.description: The coefficient of restitution of the actor. The coefficient of restitution is the ratio of the final to initial relative between two bodies/actors when they collide. In other words, restitution is th

parameters.name: ccd
parameters.label: Continuous Collision Detection
parameters.description: Toggles continuous collision detection on/off for this actor. Typically, collision detection is done discretely, meaning that collision is verified at the beginning/end of a frame. However, if a body

parameters.name: dispguide
parameters.label: Display Guide
parameters.description: Toggles on the display for the collision shape in the viewer.

parameters.name: bulletfb
parameters.label: Bullet Feedback CHOP
parameters.description: A reference to a from which to feedback. The Actor will read transformation and velocity data (in the correct format, see Bullet Solver for more information) from the , and overwrite the current value

parameters.name: flextype
parameters.label: Flex Type
parameters.description: The type of dynamic Flex actor.

parameters.name: emit
parameters.label: Enable Emission
parameters.description: When enabled, the Actor will actively emit particles.

parameters.name: emitspeed
parameters.label: Emission Speed
parameters.description: The speed the particles come out of the emitter.

parameters.name: emitmax
parameters.label: Max Emission Particles
parameters.description: Sets the maximum number of particles in the Actor . Once this number is reached, emission will be done by recycling existing particles in the Actor .

parameters.name: flexposfb
parameters.label: Position Feedback TOP
parameters.description: A reference to a to feedback position. The should be encoded with the position data that will be used to override position in the simulation. The texture data will be read to correspond with the Flex

parameters.name: flexvelfb
parameters.label: Velocity Feedback TOP
parameters.description: A reference to a to feedback velocity. The should be encoded with the velocity data that will be used to override velocity in the simulation. The texture data will be read to correspond with the Flex

parameters.name: xord

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: This field allows you to change the size of an uniformly along the three axes.

parameters.name: parentxformsrc
parameters.label: Parent Transform Source
parameters.description: Select what position is used as the transform source for this obejct. Can be one of "Parent (Hierarchy)", "Specify Parent Object", or "World Origin".

parameters.name: parentobject
parameters.label: Parent Object
parameters.description: Allows the location of the object to be constrained to any other object whose path is specified in this parameter.

parameters.name: lookat
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to orient this by naming another 3D you would like it to , or point to. Once you have designated this to look at, it will continue to face that , even if you move it. This is useful if, for

parameters.name: forwarddir

parameters.description: Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

parameters.name: lookup

parameters.name: pathsop
parameters.label: Path SOP
parameters.description: Names the that functions as the path you want this to move along. For instance, you can name a that provides a path for the camera to follow.

parameters.name: roll
parameters.label: Roll
parameters.description: Using the angle control you can specify a 's rotation as it animates along the path.

parameters.name: pos
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: This parameter lets you specify the of the along the path. The values you can enter for this parameter range from to , where equals the starting point and equals the end point of the path. The value s

parameters.name: pathorient
parameters.label: Orient along Path
parameters.description: If this option is selected, the will be oriented along the path. The positive Z axis of the will be pointing down the path.

parameters.name: bank
parameters.label: Auto-Bank Factor
parameters.description: The rolls the based on the curvature of the path at its current position. To turn off auto-banking, set the bank scale to .
